title: Transitions, Filters, Liquid, Spring & Anchor
description: Five native web platform patterns: shared-element morphs, @starting-style filters, SVG liquid text, spring drag, and anchor-positioned segmented controls.

drops.title: View Transitions Morph
drops.description: Click a card and watch it smoothly morph to a fullscreen detail view using the CSS View Transitions API.
drops.concept: The View Transitions API (document.startViewTransition()) captures old and new DOM states, then animates between them using CSS pseudo-elements. Assigning matching view-transition-name values on the card image and title creates shared-element transitions that animate size, position, and opacity simultaneously — no FLIP math or manual keyframes needed.
drops.source: Chrome Developers / MDN Blog — View Transitions API

interaction.mouse: Click any card to open detail view
interaction.click: Back button returns to grid with reverse transition

drops.title: @starting-style Filter Grid
drops.description: Filter a grid where items animate in/out of display:none using @starting-style and transition-behavior: allow-discrete.
drops.concept: CSS @starting-style defines the 'from' state for elements transitioning out of display:none, solving a long-standing impossibility. Combined with transition-behavior: allow-discrete, CSS can now animate discrete properties like display and visibility — enabling true enter/exit animations with zero JavaScript. A stagger option adds per-item transition-delay for orchestrated reveal.
drops.source: Chrome for Developers — Four new CSS features for smooth entry and exit animations

interaction.mouse: Click filter pills to show/hide categories
interaction.click: Toggle stagger checkbox for orchestrated vs simultaneous entrance

drops.title: SVG Liquid Text
drops.description: SVG feTurbulence + feDisplacementMap turns any text into flowing liquid — 5 presets and 6 live sliders.
drops.concept: The SVG filter pipeline chains feTurbulence (which generates procedural Perlin/fractal noise) into feDisplacementMap (which shifts each pixel by the noise value scaled by a displacement factor). Animating baseFrequency over time creates the illusion of text rippling through liquid. The technique works on any SVG text, scales to any size, and requires no canvas or images.
drops.source: SVG Filter Specification — W3C / MDN SVG Filter Primitives

interaction.mouse: Drag sliders to tune displacement, frequency, octaves
interaction.click: Preset buttons switch filter type; click hero SVG to pause/resume

drops.title: Spring Rubber-Band Drag
drops.description: Drag any card — it stretches with rubber-band resistance then snaps back with a CSS linear() spring easing curve.
drops.concept: While dragging, the card's transform is set directly via pointer events for immediate tactile feedback. The rubber-band effect divides displacement by a resistance exponent, so the card fights back proportionally. On release, a CSS transition takes over using a hand-crafted linear() timing function with overshoot points baked in — the GPU animates the spring physics entirely in CSS without a JS animation loop.
drops.source: ICS Media — Using CSS linear() for spring animations in UI

interaction.mouse: Drag any card to stretch it; release to snap back
interaction.click: Adjust resistance, rotation, perspective and bounce sliders
interaction.touch: Touch-drag cards with the same spring behaviour

drops.title: Anchor-Positioned Segmented Control
drops.description: The sliding pill uses CSS Anchor Positioning to follow the active option without getBoundingClientRect.
drops.concept: CSS Anchor Positioning (anchor-name / position-anchor) allows an element to reference another element's geometry directly in CSS, eliminating the need to measure DOM elements in JavaScript. The sliding background pill anchors to the active option's bounding box, and a spring linear() transition on left and width produces the characteristic iOS-style overshoot. Four variants demonstrate basic, emoji, vertical, and size-scaled controls.
drops.source: Samuel Kraft — Segmented Control for web + ICS Media CSS linear() spring

interaction.mouse: Click any segment option to slide the pill
interaction.click: Pick pill color with the color swatches in demo 4
interaction.touch: Tap options or swipe horizontally to change selection
interaction.keyboard: Arrow keys navigate between options (ARIA tab role)
